notes: Huge RMS + helios HBM-bound (~80-83% of 4.8 TB/s peak). Small shapes CPU launch/dispatch-bound (~32us; rmsnorm via register_custom_op + Triton).
RMS only; helios fell back. Superseded by normv2 (adds LN kernel).

correctness: 33/33; helios LN 2.86e-6 vs baseline & fp32 ref (within 1e-5)
ncu: rms_huge_cand 70.65% DRAM (1056 blocks) vs base 83.15%; ln_cand 74.22% DRAM (660 blocks, capped) vs base 79.73%
diagnosis: occupancy-capped grid-stride starved bandwidth on the big shapes (too few resident blocks -> low memory-level parallelism)
notes: Led to the launch-policy experiments below.

findings: normv3 (both uncapped, one-worker-per-row): helios 1.065x (LN DRAM 660->8640 blocks lifted to 79.8%), but huge RMS dropped to 1.00x (81090 blocks added launch/tail overhead for tiny-per-row work). normv4 (RMS capped + LN uncapped): all shapes >=1.02x, geomean 1.408x. Conclusion: LN (heavy per-block) wants one-CTA-per-row; RMS (tiny per-row) wants persistent capped grid-stride.

kept: separate launch policy for bandwidth-bound vs launch-bound (already done)
notes: KernelWiki submodule not initialized in this worktree; Codex used a fallback clone. The 128-bit two-rows-per-warp lever was applied in normv5.

src: rms_norm_d128.cuh (2-rows-per-warp, 16 lanes/row, 128-bit packed loads, warp_reduce<16>, weight in regs, capped grid-stride; odd-row tail via dummy-load+conditional-store) + layer_norm_n5120.cuh (one-CTA-per-row, exact 256x20 float4 tiling, fp32 two-moment block reduce) + norm_dispatch.py
build: SGLang jit_kernel/tvm-ffi load_jit from workspace .cuh (absolute path), no --use_fast_math
correctness: 201/201 full regression grid (incl odd M=33,257) candidate-vs-baseline + fp32 ref + select01 oracle + NaN/Inf; helios LN 2.86e-6 (==baseline vs fp32 ref, within 1e-5); bf16 within 5e-2

ncu.rms_huge_cand: 77.54% DRAM, 83.1us (baseline 83.15%/76.8us) -> near bound, ~93% of baseline BW; 2-rows-per-warp lifted it from 70.65%

ac9: integrated dispatcher path 14.93us vs baseline 31.5us (2.1x); guard overhead 2.57us (no ~5us fixed tax)
decision: Promote: parity+ on the 3 HBM-bound shapes (at/near bound), ~1.9x on the 3 launch-bound shapes. Residual huge-RMS 77.5% vs 83% DRAM documented; not chased (baseline already ~83% of peak, wall already parity+).

mechanism: kda_kernels overlay + runtime install() (monkey-patch); .cuh compiled via SGLang jit_kernel/tvm-ffi load_jit

optimized_wrapper disambiguates on weight/bias vs w kwargs, raises on ambiguity

finding: [P2] generic register()['callable'] optimized_wrapper misrouted norm_infer(x, weight, bias, eps=...) (3 positional + eps kwarg) to triton_one_pass_rms_norm
fix: route to norm_infer when len(args)==3 and 'eps' in kwargs (one-pass RMS passes eps positionally, never as kwarg, so this is unambiguous)
validation: local routing test 8/8 forms + ambiguity raise; H200 404/404 correctness; installed optimized_wrapper(x,weight,bias,eps=) == norm_infer (torch.equal True); install(strict) both swapped
consequence: re-exported _impls/register.py (stamp -> 77a791b4b export-source); reworded lineage metadata lag-proof + kernel-anchored (KDA_COMMIT_*=export-source/git-HEAD-at-export; KDA_BENCHMARKED_COMMIT_* full SHA = perf anchor, kernels byte-identical since 149392da2)
notes: No kernel/dispatch/perf change; geomean 1.4223x unchanged. optimized_wrapper is the generic callable, not the install-path (dispatcher uses wrapper->norm_dispatch directly), but the fix keeps the public API fully compatible.

findings: [P2] tests/test_correctness.py make_cases() dereferenced torch=None at pytest collection (@parametrize evaluated before module-skip) -> AttributeError on non-CUDA machines

fixes: make_cases() returns [] when torch is None (skip-safe parametrize)
wrapper.py relative-first import: `from .norm_dispatch import ...` with absolute fallback
validation: torch=None module-load sim -> make_cases()==[] no crash; H200 import kda_kernels...._impls.h200.wrapper by package path with NO sys.path mutation (norm_dispatch resolves as co-located submodule); 404/404 correctness; install(strict) swaps both norm symbols
consequence: re-exported _impls/h200/wrapper.py (stamp -> 613f780dd export-source); re-added lag-proof kernel-anchored lineage
